charters.title: Spelling Bee Game
charters.objective: Build a custom web-based Spelling Bee game with a 5-minute timer and daily scoreboard, shareable via link.
charters.successCriteria: Playable game with daily puzzles, enforced 5-min timer, nickname-based leaderboard, scoring by word length + obscurity.

decisions.text: Project initiated
5-minute timer (vs NYT untimed)
Web app format for max shareability
Nicknames for scoreboard identity
Scoring = length + obscurity
Hosting TBD — prototype first
Design approved: Vanilla JS + Express + SQLite

risks.risk: Word list too large / too small
risks.mitigation: Test with multiple dictionaries, tune
Rate limiting, optional nickname validation
Algorithm tuning, difficulty testing

charters.title: Substack to TikTok Pipeline

Approval — David reviews and approves scripts before rendering.

Short-form only (30-90s)
CTA drives to Substack, not website
Clone David's voice via ElevenLabs
David reviews scripts before rendering
Node.js ESM + Python (Pillow) + ffmpeg stack
macOS Samantha TTS as interim voice

Test ElevenLabs vs stock voices early
Use royalty-free sources, Pexels/Pixabay

charters.title: Mini Crossword
charters.objective: Build a daily mini crossword game for www.neuralnexus.press/crossword — a 5×5 grid with science/tech-themed clues, inspired by the NYT Mini. Quick to play (2-5 min), shareable results, daily rotation.
charters.successCriteria: - 5×5 crossword grid with across/down clues

decisions.text: 5×5 grid (not 7×7 or larger)
Pre-authored puzzles, no auto-generation
Client-side only, no server API
Science/tech vocabulary focus
Words don't need to be science-themed

risks.mitigation: Pre-author puzzles manually, no auto-generation needed
Custom on-screen keyboard, suppress native
Curate science-themed words, review each puzzle

charters.title: SEO & Analytics Strategy

Option A approved
Platform architecture decided

risks.mitigation: Use canonical tags pointing to neuralnexus.press as primary

risks.risk: Low domain authority limits ranking potential
risks.mitigation: Focus on low-competition long-tail keywords first
Target "free daily [game]" long-tail, not head terms

Consider Plausible/Umami as supplement

charters.title: Oral Supplementation

Phase 1 supplements all arrived and started

decisions.text: Charter created
Scope expanded to include off-label pharma + injectables + novel pathways
Compound review completed
Shopping list produced
Novel pathways research completed
Off-label pharmaceuticals assessed

David's top 3 potent compounds selected

Only recommend third-party tested brands (NSF/USP)

risks.mitigation: Keep daily stack ≤8 supplements; simple AM/PM split

charters.title: Cognitive Performance Dashboard
charters.objective: Build a longitudinal cognitive performance measurement system that combines standardized testing, chess ELO tracking, and Neural NeXus game analytics — with supplement/intervention phase markers — to objectively evaluate whether cognitive enhancement strategies are working.
charters.successCriteria: - [ ] Standardized cognitive battery built and deployed on neuralnexus.press

outstanding.text: Analytics with longitudinal charts + Cohen's d

decisions.text: Three-track measurement system adopted
Chess ELO as primary passive metric
Neural NeXus games as supplementary only
Standardized battery: dual n-back + PVT + DSST
2-week baseline required before any new intervention

risks.risk: Practice effects inflate standardized test scores
risks.mitigation: Use adaptive difficulty (n-back); track asymptote, not raw score
Keep battery under 3 min; gamify with streaks

risks.risk: Chess ELO influenced by non-cognitive factors (tilt, fatigue)
risks.mitigation: Large sample size per window; flag outlier sessions

risks.risk: Puzzle difficulty variance in NN games
risks.mitigation: 14-day rolling average; don't use for primary decisions
Pre-register expected effect before starting compound; blind analysis where possible

charters.title: Mobile Cognitive App (PWA)

decisions.text: PWA over native app
Telegram cron for reminders (iOS lacks Web Push)
Bottom tab bar navigation

programs.name: Treat Biosciences
programs.description: Canine therapeutic development — TRT-001 triple incretin agonist for canine obesity.

charters.title: Triple Incretin Program (TRT-001)
charters.objective: Canine-optimized triple incretin agonist modeled on retatrutide. Structural analysis: 80-90%+ probability unmodified retatrutide works. GLP-1R 93.1%, GIPR 100%, GCGR 100% conserved at binding contacts.
charters.successCriteria: - [x] Competitive landscape analysis complete (updated 2026-03-19 with Okava, Teva generic, APOP)

outstanding.text: Lilly retatrutide patent coverage for veterinary

risks.mitigation: Design diverse candidates; use proven scaffolds
David can do Phase 0 solo; recruit new team if needed
Multiple paths (accelerator + SBIR + angels + bootstrap)
Speed to patent + conditional approval
Re-file incorporation if needed ($500)

charters.title: TRT-002 In Silico Design Pipeline
charters.phase: Track 2: AF3 VALIDATION COMPLETE — 9/9 pass (ipTM 0.75-0.87). STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS in progress. Track 1: Parked, awaiting David GO on gene synthesis.
charters.objective: Two-track drug design pipeline for TRT-002. Track 1: Engineered follistatin fragment (ND-FSD1-FSD2, 212 aa canine) as myostatin/activin antagonist. Track 2: De novo dual ActRIIA/ActRIIB competitive blocker (bimagrumab MOA) designed from Morvan crystal structures (2.0Å).
charters.successCriteria: ELISA IC50 < 100 nM myostatin inhibition = GO. Gene synthesis order placed, binding data in 6 weeks.

blockers.text: David GO/NO-GO for GenScript gene synthesis order (~$400)

outstanding.text: Phase 6: Expert panel review (Hypatia + Socrates) — GO WITH CONDITIONS, all met
Track 2: Dual ActRII blocker design — review complete, BindCraft execution pending
Track 2: Execute BindCraft campaigns on Colab
Track 2: Resolve final conditions from panel → v4 final → BindCraft execution
Download BindCraft R1 PDB/CSV outputs from Colab (backed up to Drive)
Panel triage of 23 R1 designs → 9 advancing from 5 scaffolds
AF3 server validation of top 9 BindCraft R1 designs (in progress)
David selects final candidates for gene synthesis
Track 2: AF3 binary validation — 9/9 pass, controls establish dynamic range (0.11-0.84)
Track 2: Panel review of AF3 report — Hypatia GO, Socrates GO WITH CONDITIONS (folded in)
Track 2: Structural analysis — steric clash overlay + contact mapping
Track 2: Day 2 AF3 — re-seed top designs + ternary fold

decisions.text: Construct A (ND-FSD1-FSD2, 212 aa) is lead drug candidate
E. coli SHuffle T7 primary, Pichia backup
V7 robust (N82Q+R140Q+R169Q) strongest canine variant
PRODIGY validated for this target system
Ship imperfect drug — fix with data after ELISA proof
Sequence corrected: 215aa human → 212aa canine backbone
AF3 validates TRT-002a matches WT follistatin control
Expert panel GO WITH CONDITIONS — all conditions resolved
Track 2 (BindCraft de novo) approved in parallel
Track 2 pivoted: myostatin-specific binder → dual ActRII competitive blocker (bimagrumab MOA)
Design basis: Morvan et al. PNAS 2017 crystal structures (ActRIIA 2.35Å, ActRIIB 2.00Å)
Canine ActRIIB ECD = 100% identical to human (116/116 aa) — zero species translation risk
Charter 703 Track 2 review complete: Hypatia CONDITIONAL GO, Socrates IMPROVED/HIGH-RISK
BindCraft R1: 23 accepted de novo ActRIIB binder designs from ~60 trajectories (36% acceptance rate), lengths 80-120 aa
Big funnel strategy: maximize designs → confirmed binders → optimize from data (David's direction)
Panel triage complete: 9 designs from 5 scaffolds advance to AF3 validation. Top: l114_s404474_mpnn3 (i_pTM 0.83, dG -63.7). Best compact: l83_s36931_mpnn9 (83 aa, 1 Met)
AF3 validation 9/9 PASS — ipTM range 0.75-0.87 (mean 0.82). Best: s666280_mpnn20 (ipTM 0.87)
AF3 controls confirm gate validity: N1 scrambled ipTM 0.11 (floor), N2 rejected design ipTM 0.79 (ipTM alone insufficient), P3 bimagrumab Fv ipTM 0.84 (gold standard)
ActRIIB selectivity confirmed: lead design vs BMPRII ipTM 0.49 — addresses ACE-031 off-target safety concern

charters.title: ExoDev Protocol Optimization (Apollo 3-5)
charters.objective: Optimize whole embryo culture (WEC) protocols for Apollo 3-5 Foxtrot. Address VYS swelling, yolk sac circulation failure, and cardiovascular compromise in extended culture.
charters.successCriteria: Successful VYS vascular remodeling and cardiovascular development through gastrulation window.

blockers.text: Okolab H4 humidifier heater failure (H4.a + H4.d errors) — support email being drafted for Monday. Currently using water bottle bypass.

outstanding.text: Key insight: NAC likely harming VYS vascular development (ROS required for HIF-1α/VEGF signaling)
NAC + ITS-X dual antioxidant assault identified during critical 48hr gastrulation window
Recommendation: drop NAC, keep ITS-X (metabolic support > antioxidant harm)
Protocol issues: NAC concentration unspecified, unit error persists
Group 4 design needed for synergy testing (Hypothesis 2.1.3)
Validate NAC removal improves VYS outcomes in next protocol run
E8.5 roller culture run in progress (2026-03-28) — Okolab H4 heater failed, bypass deployed

decisions.text: VYS swelling identified as primary failure mode
NAC paradox: antioxidant suppresses ROS-dependent vascular signaling (HIF-1α, VEGF)
Drop NAC, keep ITS-X — metabolic support without vascular harm
Okolab H4 humidifier heater failure — water bottle bypass + enclosure setpoint adjustment
